steps.title: Extract f(x) values and identify the axis of symmetry
steps.content:
From $y = f(x) + 4$, we have $f(x) = y - 4$. Converting each table value:
  • At $x = 21$: $f(21) = -8 - 4 = -12$
  • At $x = 23$: $f(23) = 8 - 4 = 4$
  • At $x = 25$: $f(25) = -8 - 4 = -12$
Since $f(21) = f(25) = -12$, the parabola is symmetric between $x = 21$ and $x = 25$. The axis of symmetry is at the midpoint: $$x = \frac{21 + 25}{2} = 23$$ The vertex is at $(23, 4)$.

steps.title: Set up vertex form and find the leading coefficient
